initiatives.title: Undergraduate GenAI research program
initiatives.description: Harvard's Generative AI Research Program funds undergraduates to work with faculty on focused GenAI research projects across disciplines.
initiatives.significance: Shows an R1 institution moving AI into governed practice, research capacity, teaching, or workforce preparation.

initiatives.title: AI Sandbox campus expansion and usage milestone
initiatives.description: Harvard expanded AI Sandbox to students and reported a 2024-25 milestone of 10,795 users and 3.8 million chats, providing a rare public usage metric for an enterprise AI environment.
initiatives.significance: The usage metric closes an outcomes/platform gap that is often opaque at private R1 institutions.

investment.description: Harvard publishes usage metrics but not AI Sandbox contract or operating cost.

collaborations.description: The public HUIT page presents AI Sandbox as a Harvard service; underlying model vendors are not itemized on the cited page.

initiatives.policy_implications: Provides a reviewed environment for AI use with Harvard data-handling guardrails.

initiatives.title: $500M Kempner Institute and AI compute capacity
initiatives.description: Harvard launched the Kempner Institute with a reported $500 million Chan Zuckerberg Initiative gift, and FAS Research Computing lists 528 GPUs for Kempner AI cluster resources.
initiatives.significance: Combines disclosed philanthropic scale with public compute-capacity details.

investment.description: Gift establishing the Kempner Institute for natural and artificial intelligence research.

collaborations.description: Philanthropic founding support for the institute.

computing_infrastructure.supercomputer_name: Kempner AI Cluster / FAS Research Computing GPUs
computing_infrastructure.summary: Harvard FAS Research Computing documentation lists GPU resources for the Kempner AI cluster, including 384 H100 GPUs and 144 A100 GPUs; this count excludes other Harvard GPU resources not itemized in the same page.

narrative_summary: Harvard University shows an AI posture with five documented initiatives across 4 categories. The evidence emphasizes University-wide GenAI working groups, FAS Generative AI initiative, and EVP AI Innovation Program. Policy maturity is coded as comprehensive based on public guidance, privacy language, governance, or tool controls. The profile also records teaching, research, workforce, equity, or enterprise-tool activity where sources support it. Leadership names, enrollment, GPU counts, and contract values are left null when not found in the cited public sources.
